Description: Iraqi forces invade Kuwait; fears of a wider Gulf supply disruption trigger a sharp price spike.

Event: Gulf War - Operation Desert Storm begins
Description: US-led coalition launches military action to expel Iraq from Kuwait.
Collapse of the Thai baht triggers a regional financial crisis that sharply reduces Asian oil demand.
Combination of Asian demand collapse and OPEC overproduction pushes prices to decade lows.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers agree to cut output by ~2.1 million bpd, helping prices recover through 1999-2000.
Attacks on the US create short-term demand uncertainty and market volatility.
Coalition forces invade Iraq, raising concerns about Middle East supply stability.

Event: Brent crude peaks near record high (~$147/bbl)
Description: Strong global demand, a weak US dollar, and speculative activity drive prices to an all-time high.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggers a global financial crisis and demand collapse, crashing oil prices within months.
Widespread political unrest across the Middle East and North Africa, including Libyan civil war, disrupts regional oil supply.

Event: Rise of ISIS in Iraq / early stage of US shale boom
Description: Militant advances in Iraq raise supply-risk premiums while rapidly rising US shale output builds a supply glut.

Event: OPEC declines to cut production (Vienna meeting)
Description: OPEC opts to maintain output despite falling prices, seen as a strategy to defend market share against US shale, accelerating the price collapse.
Persistent oversupply combined with a China growth slowdown pushes prices to a 12-year low.
OPEC agrees with non-OPEC producers (led by Russia) to cut output for the first time since 2008, supporting a price recovery.

Event: US withdraws from Iran nuclear deal
Description: US reinstates sanctions on Iranian oil exports, tightening expected global supply.
OPEC+ talks collapse; Saudi Arabia launches a price war by ramping up output, crashing prices amid emerging COVID-19 demand fears.

Event: WTI futures turn negative (COVID-19 demand collapse)
Description: COVID-19 lockdowns collapse global oil demand and storage capacity nears its limit, briefly sending US futures prices negative.

Event: OPEC+ agrees record production cuts (9.7 million bpd)
Description: OPEC+ agrees to the largest coordinated production cut in history in response to the COVID-19 demand shock.
Russia's invasion and the resulting Western sanctions on Russian energy exports raise severe global supply concerns.
